title: Metal plate workers, smiths, moulders and related occupations
description: Construct, assemble, maintain, and repair stationary steam boilers and boiler house auxiliaries. Align structures or plate sections to assemble boiler frame tanks or vats, following blueprints. Work involves use of hand and power tools, plumb bobs, levels, wedges, dogs, or turnbuckles. Assist in testing assembled vessels. Direct cleaning of boilers and boiler furnaces. Inspect and repair boiler fittings, such as safety valves, regulators, automatic-control mechanisms, water columns, and auxiliary machines. Position and secure steel bars or mesh in concrete forms in order to reinforce concrete. Use a variety of fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, and hand tools. Includes rod busters. Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products and equipment, such as ducts, control boxes, drainpipes, and furnace casings. Work may involve any of the following: setting up and operating fabricating machines to cut, bend, and straighten sheet metal; shaping metal over anvils, blocks, or forms using hammer; operating soldering and welding equipment to join sheet metal parts; or inspecting, assembling, and smoothing seams and joints of burred surfaces. Includes sheet metal duct installers who install prefabricated sheet metal ducts used for heating, air conditioning, or other purposes. (Based on 30 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Clean and smooth molds, cores, and core boxes, and repair surface imperfections.
Remove solidified steel or slag from pouring nozzles, using long bars or oxygen burners.
Position and secure workpieces on machines, and start feeding mechanisms.
Set up, operate, or tend metal or plastic molding, casting, or coremaking machines to mold or cast parts.
Pour molten metal into molds, manually or with crane ladles.
Lift upper mold sections from lower sections, and remove molded patterns.
Sprinkle or spray parting agents onto patterns and mold sections to facilitate removal of patterns from molds.
Sift and pack sand into mold sections, core boxes, and pattern contours, using hand or pneumatic ramming tools.
Cut spouts, runner holes, and sprue holes into molds.
Measure and visually inspect products for surface and dimension defects to ensure conformance to specifications.
Examine molds to ensure they are clean, smooth, and properly coated.
Operate ovens or furnaces to bake cores or to melt, skim, and flux metal.
Move and position workpieces, such as mold sections, patterns, and bottom boards, using cranes, or signal others to move workpieces.
Prepare material to load into furnaces, including cleaning, crushing, or applying chemicals.
Inspect and repair boiler fittings, such as safety valves, regulators, and automatic-control mechanisms.
Assemble and embed cores in casting frames, using hand tools and equipment.
Load specified amounts of metal and flux into furnaces or clay crucibles.
Turn valves to circulate water through cores, or spray water on filled molds to cool and solidify metal.
Attach rigging and signal crane or hoist operators to lift heavy frame and plate sections or other parts into place.
Clean pressure vessel equipment using scrapers, wire brushes, and cleaning solvents.
Inspect assembled vessels or individual components to locate any defects.
Bolt or arc weld pressure vessel structures and parts together, using wrenches or welding equipment.
Examine boilers, pressure vessels, tanks, or vats to locate defects, such as leaks or weak spots, for repair.
Transport metal ingots to storage areas, using forklifts.
Lay out plate, sheet steel, or other heavy metal and mark bending and cutting lines using protractors and templates.
Repair or replace defective pressure vessel parts, such as safety valves or regulators, using torches and other tools.
Regulate supplies of fuel and air, or control flow of electric current and water coolant to heat furnaces.
Fabricate ducts for high efficiency heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems to maximize efficiency.
Record production data, and maintain production logs.
Shape or fabricate parts, such as stacks or chutes, using heavy-metalworking machines.
Install assemblies, such as pipes and ducts, in supportive frameworks.
Study blueprints to determine locations, relationships, or dimensions of parts.
Conduct pressure tests on vessels, such as boilers.
Transport prefabricated parts to construction sites for assembly and installation.
Measure, mark, and scribe metal or plastic stock to lay out machining, using protractors or rulers.
Shape seams, joints, or irregular edges of pressure vessel sections to attain specified fit using cutting tools.
Inspect workpieces for defects and measure them to determine accuracy of machine operation, using rules, templates, or other measuring instruments.
Observe machine operations to detect workpiece defects or machine malfunctions, adjusting machines as necessary and ensuring conformance to specifications.
Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications, tooling instructions, and plan operational sequences for machine setups.
Set up and operate machines to make metallic and plastic workpieces, including selecting, installing, and adjusting the alignment of cutting tools.
Perform minor electrical and mechanical repairs and adjustments to machines while notifying supervisors of needed major service.
Remove burrs, sharp edges, rust, or scale from workpieces, and ensure proper lubrication and cooling during machining operations.
Instruct other workers on machine set-up and operation as well as support metalworking projects from planning through assembly and testing.
Monitor machine feed and speed during operations and record operational data such as pressure readings, lengths of strokes, and speeds.
Align and secure holding fixtures, cutting tools, and workpieces on machines, ensuring accurate measurements and alignment using various instruments.
Lay out, measure, and mark metal stock for cutting and processing, applying knowledge of metal properties and machine operations.
Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, or turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant against tools and workpieces.
Compute machine indexings and settings for specified dimensions and base reference points, using knowledge of metal properties and shop mathematics.
Repair or replace machine parts using hand tools, and notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required.
Position, secure, and align cutting tools in toolholders on machines, verifying their positions with measuring instruments.
Start machines and set controls to regulate vacuum, air pressure, sizing rings, and temperature, and to synchronize speed of extrusion.
Install holding fixtures, cams, gears, and stops to control stock and tool movement, using hand tools, power tools, and measuring instruments.
Monitor machine cycles and mill operation to detect jamming and ensure that products conform to specifications.
Troubleshoot, maintain, and make minor repairs to equipment and perform preventative maintenance on machines.
Read rolling orders, blueprints, and mill schedules to determine setup specifications, work sequences, product dimensions, and installation procedures.
Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments, templates, and fixtures.
